title: Deleting a container in a pod doesn't send a pod event
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When a podman container in a pod is removed there is not pod event sent out to inform a client to refresh it's pod state. This causes a bug in cockpit-podman, the pod state is not updated and therefore the pod removal dialog will show that it will remove the already removed container.

https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/issues/1075

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pod --create testpod

2. podman create --pod test --rm --name test1 archlinux -d

3. podman rm test1

**Describe the results you received:**

We receive a container removal event, but no pod event.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Either the container removal event references the pod it is part of or a separate pod event is sent.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
4.2.0
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body: Would annotating the container removal event that the container was in a pod be equivalent from your eyes, or do you definitely want a separate event?

> Would annotating the container removal event that the container was in a pod be equivalent from your eyes, or do you definitely want a separate event?

That would work, now we have as workaround to always update the pod state when a container is removed so only doing it for containers in pods would fine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Just tested the code, it works here locally with podman `main`. However I noticed that we receive two `start` events one without `podId` and one after with `podId`. So this would not allow us update the information for a specific pod only.

This seems to also happen podman 4.2.1 which lacks the `podId` change (two events), also for removal. So this seems like a different kind of bug. First one having a healthStatus and the second one doesn't, that seems to be the only difference.

```json
{
    "status": "start",
    "id": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
    "from": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
    "Type": "container",
    "Action": "start",
    "Actor": {
        "ID": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
        "Attributes": {
            "containerExitCode": "0",
            "image": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
            "name": "zealous_kepler"
        }
    },
    "scope": "local",
    "time": 1664460601,
    "timeNano": 1664460601257289500,
    "HealthStatus": ""
}
```

```json
{
    "status": "start",
    "id": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
    "from": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
    "Type": "container",
    "Action": "start",
    "Actor": {
        "ID": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
        "Attributes": {
            "containerExitCode": "0",
            "image": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
            "name": "zealous_kepler",
            "podId": "66c5200975e565cfd959531128c352177dc86fc81a2504f799dd0297491510c5"
        }
    },
    "scope": "local",
    "time": 1664460601,
    "timeNano": 1664460601257289500
}
```

Tested with: `./bin/podman run --rm -d --pod reload_pod -ti archlinux  sleep 5`

It's the same for the remove event:

```json
{
    "status": "remove",
    "id": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
    "from": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
    "Type": "container",
    "Action": "remove",
    "Actor": {
        "ID": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
        "Attributes": {
            "containerExitCode": "0",
            "image": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
            "name": "zealous_kepler"
        }
    },
    "scope": "local",
    "time": 1664460606,
    "timeNano": 1664460606684060200,
    "HealthStatus": ""
}
```

```json
{
    "status": "remove",
    "id": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
    "from": "docker.io/archlinux/archlinux:latest",
    "Type": "container",
    "Action": "remove",
    "Actor": {
        "ID": "f014ddcb13c83269ccb68a3cff82a5eebc9f3b02b3e2d5c2263827800ad3d27b",
        "Attributes": {
            "containerExitCode": "0",
```

A minor note, shouldn't ite be `podID` instead of `podId` for consistency?
